strengths: Actively building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators at re:cinq, giving him rare hands-on production exposure to the agentic frameworks and tooling the role is built around.

Proven distributed-systems credibility at meaningful scale — low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, and a self-built event-driven geo-data platform on Kubernetes, directly mapping to the streaming and real-time inference architecture required.

Polyglot engineering depth spanning 15+ years across backend, frontend, mobile, and embedded systems, with fintech touchpoints at RIDE Capital (investment platform) and Optilyz (cross-country pricing optimization), demonstrating the full-stack architectural range a CTO must command.
concerns: The resume reflects a staff/senior individual-contributor track throughout; there is no demonstrated experience building, scaling, or leading engineering organizations, which is a hard requirement for a role that demands growing a team from 10 to 30+ people.

Fintech-infrastructure depth is thin — RIDE Capital tenure was 15 months with no described work in credit systems, payment flows, real-time risk, or multi-jurisdictional compliance, leaving the core domain expertise largely unverified.

Current agentic AI work is described at a contributing-engineer level (tooling, frameworks, MCP generators) rather than at the architectural ownership and strategic direction the CTO role requires across production multi-agent systems.
response.justification: Bogdan is one of a small cohort of engineers with genuine hands-on exposure to MCP and agentic workflow infrastructure in a production context, and his Dunnhumby work demonstrates that he can build and operate high-throughput distributed systems — the technical bedrock the CTO role sits on. If the company is seeking a deeply technical 'player-coach' CTO who can credibly own the agentic architecture while growing into the organizational leadership mandate, Bogdan's current trajectory makes him a compelling early-stage bet; the primary open question is whether his staff-engineer experience can translate quickly to executive team-building and board-level communication.
